studies.title: Interactive AI assistance for predictive and flexible control in discharge and transition management: Exploratory Study
studies.brief_summary:
Every day, hospitals discharge patients who require continued care in medical/nursing or rehabilitative facilities after their hospital stay. This further treatment by other care providers is organized by discharge management (DM). Currently, organization and communication with follow-up care providers is done via paper, fax, and telephone. This leads to a great deal of work for DM employees and sometimes, a loss of information, as transparency is not always guaranteed.  

The overall goal of the KIAFlex research project is to address these difficulties and thereby potentially improve DM in hospitals. The project will use artificial intelligence methods and close participation of end-users to develop functional AI assistance systems for improving DM. In this explorative part of the project, KIAFlex-Explore, it will be explored how employees in DM as well as patients and their relatives who have to plan follow-up care feel about an AI system in this respect. classification.reason: The study is described as exploratory and focuses on the development and evaluation of AI assistance systems for discharge management. There is no mention of randomization, controlled comparison, or interventional design. The lack of specific details about study type or allocation further supports classification as RWE.
